name: coupling_fep_leviathan

pytorch.reason: free energy tensors: F_collective under sovereign prior vs. sum of individual free energies

pyg.reason: not needed; no GNN in this sim

z3.reason: UNSAT: shared free energy = 0 requires agreement on sovereign prior; disagreement contradicts F=0

cvc5.reason: not needed; z3 handles shared-prior SMT proof

sympy.reason: symbolic Jensen inequality proof: sovereign average prior minimizes total KL divergence

clifford.reason: agents as grade-1 Cl(3,0) vectors; outer product gives grade-2 social contract bivector encoding shared prior

geomstats.reason: not needed; grade-2 bivector handled by Clifford algebra

e3nn.reason: not needed; no equivariant network in this sim

rustworkx.reason: star topology (Leviathan) vs clique (state of nature): star has O(N) edges vs O(N^2); efficiency verified

xgi.reason: sovereign hyperedge captures all agents in one shared constraint; individual FEP edges are pairwise; order difference is load-bearing

toponetx.reason: not needed; star topology handled by rustworkx

gudhi.reason: not needed; no persistent homology in this sim

pytorch_coordination_reduces_free_energy.claim: F_collective < sum(F_individual): Leviathan sovereign reduces total free energy

sympy_jensen_sovereign_kl.claim: Jensen inequality: KL with sovereign prior <= average of individual KLs

clifford_agents_grade1_contract_grade2.claim: agents as grade-1 Cl(3,0) vectors; social contract bivector (grade-2) encodes shared constraint plane

rustworkx_star_vs_clique.claim: Leviathan star topology requires fewer edges than state-of-nature clique; coordination is efficient

xgi_sovereign_hyperedge.claim: sovereign hyperedge (all agents co-participate) vs. individual pairwise FEP edges; Leviathan is a higher-order hyperedge

z3_fcollective_zero_disagree_unsat.claim: UNSAT: F_collective=0 requires shared prior; agents disagreeing on p makes F_collective=0 impossible

pytorch_heterogeneous_priors_higher_F.claim: heterogeneous priors always produce higher total F than sovereign average prior

sympy_kl_nonnegative_boundary.claim: F = KL(q||p) >= 0 always; equality iff q=p (agents agree on prior)

pytorch_single_agent_no_coordination_ben.claim: single agent: sovereign = agent itself; F_collective = F_individual (no benefit)

clifford_parallel_agents_degenerate_cont.claim: parallel agents (identical beliefs) have zero outer product; social contract is degenerate (trivially satisfied)

rustworkx_sovereign_no_agents_trivial.claim: sovereign with no agents has no contract edges; degenerate Leviathan (no coordination possible)

xgi_single_agent_degenerate_hyperedge.claim: single-agent hyperedge: no higher-order constraint; FEP and Leviathan are degenerate (individual = collective)

coupling_claim: FEP (individual free-energy minimization) and Leviathan (social contract = shared sovereign prior) are structurally coupled: the sovereign imposes a shared generative model p_shared that reduces total F below the sum of individual F values (Jensen inequality). UNSAT: F_collective=0 requires agents to agree on p_shared; disagreement makes F=0 impossible. Leviathan is the higher-order hyperedge that makes individual FEP agents collectively coherent.
